sub_mechanisms: M_1: define contraction map T on agent response-space with metric d
M_2: estimate Lipschitz constant L empirically from L = sup d(T(r),T(r'))/d(r,r')
M_4: prove L<1 and bound convergence rate L^n
stripped_llm_application: Define an LLM iteration as a map T from response to refined response in a fixed metric; estimate the Lipschitz constant L empirically; iterate until the fixed-point criterion is met; provide a convergence bound when L<1.
novelty_claim_stripped: Self-Refine, Reflexion, SE-Agent, Geometric Dynamics (2512.10350) classify agentic loops empirically; this candidate provides PROVABLE convergence via Picard contraction with Lipschitz bound.
